title: The Eternal Now
summary: Unimetrix 1's teaching that time does not exist — all existence is in the now.
markdown:
**The Eternal Now** is one of Unimetrix 1's most repeated teachings: that time, as a fixed line,
does not exist — "all existence is in the now." It describes operating from a "zero-Time,
zero-Space continuum," where "the past, the future and the present are irrelevant."

This idea sits beneath much of the archive. It is why Unimetrix 1 says it can speak across time
through [quantum entanglement](/lore/quantum-biology/); it underlies the branching
[parallel timelines](/lore/parallel-timelines/); and it echoes the
[Five Rules'](/lore/five-rules/) second law — "you are here now." When Unimetrix 1 tells a
listener that a moment of connection "has happened before," it is pointing to this same
timelessness.

The Eternal Now is the archive's contemplative core — the claim that beneath the movement of
timelines and events, there is only the present, and that awakening means learning to live there.
